issues: The draft frames this as generic 'Panthers roster statistics and game recap' without flagging the Sockeyes-relevant content: Dave Tomlinson (1987 Richmond Sockeyes player) is explicitly mentioned as being returned to Cincinnati after brief Panthers appearances.
The short description does not mention Dave Tomlinson, who is the primary reason this clipping is archive-relevant.
The long description buries Dave Tomlinson in a list of notes rather than foregrounding him as the Sockeyes connection.

Randy Moller mentioned in the long description is NOT Bryon Moller of the Sockeyes — this is a different person (Florida Panthers defenceman Randy Moller); the draft does not conflate them, but the archive should be aware this is a coincidental name match and not a Sockeyes connection.
No headline was present in the OCR, which the draft correctly left blank.
No byline was present in the OCR, which the draft correctly left blank.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: South Florida Sun Sentinel Florida Panthers statistics page (March 5, 1995) noting that ex-Richmond Sockeyes forward Dave Tomlinson was returned to the Cincinnati (IHL) affiliate after brief NHL action.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: This page from the South Florida Sun Sentinel (March 5, 1995, p. 42) carries full Florida Panthers player and goaltender statistics through 21 games of the 1994–95 NHL season. A game-notes column confirms that centre Dave Tomlinson — a 1987 Richmond Sockeyes alumnus — was returned to the Cincinnati Cyclones (IHL) after playing only a few shifts against Ottawa and sitting out his final two games as a Panther. The page also includes brief recaps of the Maple Leafs' 3-2 win over Calgary and the Canucks' 5-4 victory over Los Angeles, and notes that defenceman Randy Moller (no relation to Sockeyes) sprained his knee on his first shift.
review.notes: The primary Sockeyes relevance is Dave Tomlinson (North Vancouver, BC; 1987 Sockeyes roster) being reassigned to Cincinnati. Randy Moller mentioned here is Florida Panthers defenceman Randy Moller — a different person from Sockeyes player Bryon Moller; no conflation intended but worth noting for future searches.
